scope: Reservoir-level fluid data for Vår Energi NCS fields: BALDER, JOTUN, RINGHORNE, GOLIAT, MARULK
methodology: Web research across Sodir factpages, Norwegian Petroleum, ExxonMobil/Equinor crude assays, SPE/OnePetro papers, operator press releases, industry trade press

BALDER.operator: Vår Energi (90%) + Mime Petroleum (10%) [as of 2024]; historically Esso/ExxonMobil
BALDER.location: Utsira High, central North Sea, ~190 km west of Stavanger

reservoir.formation: Heimdal and Hermod Formations (Paleocene turbidite sandstones) + injected sand complex (Eocene)

fluid.api_gravity_range: 23-29 (varies by sample; ExxonMobil samples 26.5 Nov-2021 and 29.1 Jul-2021)
fluid.classification: heavy, naphthenic, regular sulfur crude; similar to Alba, Harding, Captain
fluid.viscosity_note: viscous; production challenging from this Heimdal/Hermod fluid
fluid.notes: Balder Blend at terminal is 25.3% Balder + 74.7% Jotun storage (post Balder X startup)

recovery.drive_mechanism: natural aquifer drive + produced water reinjection (Utsira Fm disposal)

JOTUN.operator: Vår Energi (90%) + Kistos (10%) [post Balder X]; historically Esso/Pertra
JOTUN.location: Utsira High western flank, ~25 km north of Balder

reservoir.formation: Heimdal Formation (Paleocene sandy turbidites, submarine fan)
reservoir.quality: good in west, deteriorating east (more shale)
reservoir.notes: High-porosity quartz-cemented sands shed from East Shetland Platform, deposited as high-density gravity flows on Utsira High

recovery.drive_mechanism: aquifer pressure support + gas lift; produced water disposal to Utsira Fm

RINGHORNE.operator: Vår Energi (operator post-2022 acquisition from ExxonMobil); historically Esso/ExxonMobil 100%
RINGHORNE.location: Central North Sea, tied to Balder FPSO and now Jotun FPSO

Ringhorne_Ost.drive: natural water drive from regional aquifer N-E; gas lift

fluid.api_gravity_estimate: Likely 35-40 (Statfjord-Group analogue light/medium); blended into Balder Blend; specific Ringhorne assay not publicly disclosed by Vår Energi

recovery.drive_mechanism: natural water drive + gas lift; water injection for pressure support
recovery.future_phases: Ringhorne Phase IV (Balder X), Phase V (6 new wells, 30+ mmboe), Phase VI under consideration

GOLIAT.location: Norwegian Barents Sea, ~85 km NW of Hammerfest

primary_formations: Realgrunnen Subgroup / Kapp Toscana Group (Triassic-Jurassic): Fruholmen + lower Tubåen
reservoir.structure: complex segmented; thin gas caps on each
reservoir.depositional_setting_realgrunnen: fluvial-dominated delta complex (fluvial channels, tidal channels, overbank lobes, crevasse splays, alluvial fan)

fluid.reservoir_temp_note: ~30°C (86°F) - very cool due to shallow burial in Arctic permafrost-influenced setting
fluid.gor_note: Reported from DST flow tests in Sodir well 7122/8-1S; ~95-117 Sm3/Sm3 from Kobbe Fm tests

recovery.drive_mechanism: water injection + reinjection of produced gas (~1.3 bcm/yr)

confidence.reservoir_pressures: high (Sodir + multiple secondary sources agree on 123/192 bar)
confidence.reservoir_temperature: medium (single secondary source for ~30°C; consistent with shallow burial)
confidence.gor: medium (DST-derived flow test from 7122/8-1S; production-average GOR will differ once gas reinjection equilibrated)

MARULK.operator: Vår Energi (20% operator) + Equinor Energy (33%) + INEOS E&P Norge (30%) + DNO Norge (17%)
MARULK.location: Norwegian Sea, ~30 km SW of Norne; tied back to Norne FPSO

fluid.type: gas-condensate (rich gas with condensate; minor oil)
fluid.api_gravity_condensate_estimate: Typical NCS Cretaceous gas-condensate: 50-65° API (no published Marulk-specific assay)

cross_field_observations.balder_jotun_blend: Balder export blend = 25.3% Balder + 74.7% Jotun; explains why blended terminal API can shift dramatically with Balder X / Jotun FPSO ramp-up. Pre-Balder X, the volume mix was different and the export crude was heavier.
cross_field_observations.ringhorne_into_balder: Ringhorne main + Ringhorne Øst are routed through Balder FPSO, now Jotun FPSO. Lighter Jurassic/Statfjord-sourced oil dilutes heavy Balder Heimdal/Hermod fluid.
cross_field_observations.goliat_unique: Goliat sells as standalone Goliat Blend (41.3 API, low S). Crude is much lighter than reservoir would suggest based on depth — shallow Arctic burial with cool reservoir (~30°C) preserves light fluid.
cross_field_observations.marulk_gas: Marulk is gas-condensate, not oil — recoverable oil component is tiny (0.84 mSm3). Should be excluded from oil-quality / API analyses for the decline model.

data_gaps.reservoir_temperatures: Not published for Balder, Jotun, Ringhorne, Marulk — would require operator PVT reports or SPE papers behind paywall
data_gaps.gor_for_main_fields: Production-average GOR not in public sources for Balder, Jotun, Ringhorne, Marulk; operator annual reports do not break this out per field
data_gaps.ringhorne_specific_api: No standalone Ringhorne crude assay published — only blended Balder/Jotun assays available
